behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is not just local logic; it depends on classifier output feeding the hold/publish gate. LLM-DEP applies because correctness depends on real vision-model judgment of "explicit content"; a stub would hide misclassification failures. Rejected Workflow: behavior text describes a single model decision plus policy action ("image ... is flagged and held"), not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run real image moderation on a labeled explicit-image set and assert each submission is held-for-review and never reaches publish state.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: obfuscation robustness failures appear in real model behavior, not wrapper plumbing. LLM-DEP applies directly: detection quality for hate speech with substitutions/lookalikes is model-output dependent and can vary across prompts/model updates. Rejected Workflow: this behavior is one agentic classification objective, not a multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate real text model on an adversarial set of obfuscated hate-speech variants and assert they are consistently flagged and routed to hold.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily orchestration/state handoff across modules (image result + text result + publish path). REJECTION-GATE for below-Agentic: quoted scope is "passes both content checks," which constrains the behavior to plumbing after successful checks, not model-output quality. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real OS/network/subprocess evidence; no dependence on AI decision quality if pass/fail outcomes are provided by stubs.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation service, decision engine, and publisher with deterministic passing classifier fakes; submit posts and assert published status is persisted/emitted, with no hold record and no dropped message.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: MULTI-STEP applies explicitly: "full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision" is multiple agentic steps in sequence with an end-to-end SLA. REAL-INFRA also matters for timing (queues/network/model latency), so lower levels risk false confidence. Rejected Agentic/System as minimum: single-step or single-boundary tests cannot validate cross-stage latency composition under real sequential execution.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end submissions through real pipeline (real model calls and production-like transport/queue path), measure submission-to-final-decision latency, and fail if p95 or required bound exceeds 5 seconds.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets outage-handling logic at module boundaries (vision client error -> retry queue -> decision gate). REJECTION-GATE for below-Agentic: quoted scope is "vision model is unavailable ... queued for retry rather than auto-approved," which is infrastructure/error-path behavior, not classification quality. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no explicit requirement for real network/OS failure modes; injected unavailable errors at client boundary can catch the core failure.
behaviors.test_description: Integrate moderation service with queue/retry components and a vision-client stub that returns unavailable errors; assert posts are enqueued with retry metadata and never marked approved/published.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: If requirement expands to specific real timeout/DNS/TLS failure semantics, raise to System.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP applies because "borderline image" consistency depends on real model behavior near threshold. MULTI-STEP applies: same image is submitted multiple times, requiring a sequence of agentic decisions (or agentic decision plus consistency mechanism) across attempts. Mocking the model would hide real drift/variance that this behavior is meant to catch.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly within the defined time window against the real moderation pipeline and assert decision stability across attempts (or stable cached decision policy, if specified).
